data.title: Hyzon expected to reduce workforce
data.description:
• Hydrogen fuel cell system manufacturer and technology developer Hyzon (NASDAQ:HYZN) is expected to effect a reduction in workforce.
  • HYZN shares were trading -27.71% Friday pre-market at $1.20.
  • The company cited its inability to raise funding and the future uncertainty relating to the availability of government subsidies, most notably the California Hybrid and Zero-Emission Truck and Bus Voucher Incentive Project, which is believed to have caused certain of its customers to slow down or suspend their purchasing decisions.
  • Hyzon has issued a Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act, or WARN Act, notice to employees in Bolingbrook, Illinois and Troy, and Michigan.
  • The reduction in workforce is expected to be completed in February 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Knife River enters definitive agreement to acquire Strata
data.description:
• Knife River (NYSE:KNF) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Strata, a construction materials and contracting services provider in North Dakota and northwestern Minnesota.
  • Based on Strata’s projected 2025 Adj. EBITDA, the purchase price of $454M, reflects a multiple in the high single digits.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the first half of 2025.
  • “We expect Strata to be accretive to Knife River’s Adj. EBITDA margin within the first year. Strata’s high-quality assets allow us to expand our service territory within a region we know well. We believe this is a great, long-term fit for us.” said Knife River President and CEO Brian Gray.

data.title: Humacyte surges on FDA approval for Symvess vascular repair product
data.description:
Humacyte (NASDAQ:HUMA) shares climbed ~57% in the premarket on Friday after the bioengineered tissue maker announced the FDA approval of Symvess, an off-the-shelf, implantable vascular conduit indicated for arterial injury.

According to the FDA labeling, Symvess will be available for adults with arterial injury in extremities. It will be indicated when an autologous vein graft is not feasible when there is a risk of near-term limb loss, and in other cases for urgent revascularization.

“SYMVESS approval in this first indication for arterial injury repair is a milestone for regenerative medicine overall, as well as for Humacyte,” CEO Laura Niklason remarked.

The Durham, North Carolina-based company said it has already recruited and trained sales personnel in preparation for the product’s commercial launch.

HUMA’s marketing application for Symvess is backed by real-world data and results from its V005 pivotal Phase 2/3 clinical study, which met its primary goals in patients with arterial injuries due to traumatic events such as gunshot wounds.

data.title: CCC Intelligent to acquire EvolutionIQ for $730M, effect up to $300M stock buybacks
data.description:
Software company, CCC Intelligent Solutions (NASDAQ:CCCS), is set to acquire EvolutionIQ for $730M.

EvolutionIQ is a platform for AI-powered guidance on disability and injury claims management.

The acquisition consideration is a combination of ~40% CCCS shares of common stock and 60% cash.

The cash consideration will be funded through cash on the balance sheet and an additional $225M term loan.

The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2025.

Additionally, CCC Intelligent is set to effect an up to $300M share repurchase program.

The repurchase will be funded out of available liquidity and free cash flow.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Winnebago falls after warning on subdued demand; other leisure stocks also lower
data.description:
Winnebago (NYSE:WGO) fell in early trading on Friday after the company missed consensus estimates with its FQ1 earnings report.

The company said the RV and marine operating environment remained challenging in the first quarter, marked by subdued consumer demand and a cautious dealer network reluctant to make significant commitments on new orders ahead of the historically slow winter season.

CEO Michael Happer said the company ha a strategic focus on disciplined production, effective cost management and targeted investments in new products and technologies. "These strategies, complemented by our healthy balance sheet, prudent capital spending and robust liquidity, enhance our competitive position for an anticipated market recovery in the second half of fiscal 2025," he noted.

During FQ1, revenue fell 18.0% year-over-year to $625.6 million, driven primarily by lower unit volume and a reduction in average selling price per unit related to product mix. Winnebago's (WGO) gross profit margin decreased 290 basis points during the quarter to 12.3%, reflecting deleverage, higher warranty experience compared to the prior year and product mix, partially offset by operational efficiencies. EPS was reported at -$0.03 vs. $0.20 consensus and $0.95 a year ago.

Looking ahead, Winnebago (WGO) still sees full-year revenue of $2.9 billion to $3.2 billion (midpoint $3.05 billion) vs. $2.99 billion consensus and full-year EPS of $3.10 to $4.40 (midpoint $3.75) vs. a prior view for $3.00 to $4.50 and $3.38 consensus.

Shares of Winnebago (WGO) were down 4.39% in premarket trading to $49.65. THOR Industries (THO) was down 1.83% and LCI Industries (LCII) shed 1.05%. Brunswick (BC), Polaris (PII), and BRP (DOOO) are on watch as stocks that tend to swing in the same direction as Winnebago (WGO) historically.

data.title: Taoping gets Nasdaq extension to regain compliance
data.description:
• Taoping (NASDAQ:TAOP) said it received a notice from the Nasdaq, that the firm has been granted an additional 180 calendar day period to regain compliance.
  • The firm is currently not in compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.

data.title: Robinhood competitor etoro preparing for public offering - report
data.description:
Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) competitor etoro is reportedly looking to go public and is targeting a valuation of $5 billion.

In 2021, etoro has attemped to list itself previously through a SPAC deal but had terminated it amid stock market volitality and plunge in crypto at the time. The deal had also reportedly ran into regulatory problems.

The online trading platform has significantly improved its profitability. The firm had reported a core profit of $107 million last year, a strong turnaround from the loss of over $40 million the prior year, according to a Globes report.

The rise in profitability could bode well as the market has less tolerance for unprofitable companies.

Were eToro be traded today at the same multiple, it would have a market cap of nearly $6 billion on the basis of last year’s EBITDA.

Tel Aviv-based eToro offers stock-market trading similar to that of Robinhood Markets (HOOD), and also competes with Coinbase (COIN) as a consumer crypto-trading platform.

The firm has also had a few regulatory issues, and in September, it agreed to pay $1.5 million to settle charges with the SEC.

The charges said that the company operated an unregistered broker and unregistered clearing agency in connection with its trading platform that facilitated buying and selling certain crypto assets as securities.

The firm had agreed to cease trading activity in almost all crypto assets, and the only assets that U.S. customers can trade on the company’s platform will be Bitcoin, Bitcoin Cash, and Ether.

The potential listing plans come at a time when Bitcoin (BTC-USD) has been scaling the $100K mark boosted by crypto-friendly Donald Trump's win in the presidential elections.

data.title: Applied Therapeutics appoints Johnson as Executive Chairman, CEO steps down
data.description:
• Applied Therapeutics (NASDAQ:APLT) has announced that John H. Johnson, a recognized leader in the pharmaceutical and biotechnology industry, has been named Executive Chairman.
  • Dr. Shoshana Shendelman has stepped down as Chair and CEO; and Les Funtleyder, Applied Therapeutics’ CFO, has been named Interim CEO.
  • He has served as the CEO of Reaction Biology and before that served as CEO of Strongbridge Biopharma plc prior to its acquisition by Xeris Biopharma Holdings.

data.title: ReShape Lifesciences files to sell up to 2.11M shares for holder
data.description:
• ReShape Lifesciences (NASDAQ:RSLS) has filed a prospectus to sell up to 2.11M shares of common stock for the selling stockholder.
  • The preliminary prospectus is not an offer to sell the securities.
  • FORM S-1

data.title: What investors need to know about FedEx's industry-rattling spinoff
data.description:
FedEx (NYSE:FDX) traded higher on Friday after the company announced the spinoff of its freight trucking business as part of a plan to focus on core operations. The shipping giant expects that the split of FedEx (FDX) and FedEx Freight will preserve operational synergies between both companies, while enabling greater strategic, operational and financial execution for each company and its stakeholders.

Bank of America's sum-of-the-parts value suggests significant value potential at $348 per share. FedEx Freight will retain its name and commercial collaboration with FedEx, will expand its dedicated sales force, launch an enhanced LTL-specific pricing/ invoicing system, and move to reduce vendor spend through increased automation. Analyst Ken Hoexter and his team believe the separation is set to unlock value potential as it creates the largest pure-play public North American less-than-truckload carrier.

Jefferies thinks FedEx Freight could be worth $30 billion and highlighted a $350 bull-case price target. Analyst Stephanie Moore noted that FedEx Freight is the largest less-than-truckload carrier in the U.S. with around 17% market share. Moore pointed out that the freight business is a non-union carrier similar to Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL), XPO (XPO) XPO, and Saia (SAIA). Its margin rate is better than the rates at XPO and SAIA, but not quite as good as ODFL.

Morgan Stanley is more cautious about the FedEx (FDX) news, noting the process is expected to take 18 months and investors do not know what the financials of the independent entity will look like, specifically what percentage of revenues are tied up to long-term contracts with FDX Parcel. Analyst Ravi Shanker also warned that it is unclear what the normalized margin will look like after management beefs up the sales team by hiring 300 members, brings in new tech systems, and public company corporate costs.

Analysts think the FedEx Freight announcement could have implications for a wide range of stocks including, UPS (UPS), ArcBest (ARCB), TFI International (TFI), Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL), XPO (XPO), Saia (SAIA)and Covenant Logistics Group (CVLG) due to either valuation resets or pricing implications for the LTL market.

Shares of FedEx (FDX) were up 9.20% in premarket trading to $301.27 vs. the 52-week range of $234.45 to $313.84.
